name: Flet (Flutter-Based Python Web/Desktop/Mobile Apps)

performance.notes: Flutter rendering engine; Python-to-Flutter bridge via WebSocket; web/desktop/mobile

agent_readiness.notes: Build Flutter-powered apps with pure Python. import flet as ft. def main(page: ft.Page): page.add(ft.Text('Hello')). ft.app(target=main). Components: Column, Row, Container, ElevatedButton, TextField, DataTable, ListView, Chart. Reactive: on_click, on_change callbacks. Async: async def main. Multi-user sessions. Deploy: web (flet publish), desktop (Windows/macOS/Linux), mobile (Android/iOS via Flutter). Real-time shared apps. OAuth login. Routing. Early-stage but rapidly evolving.

gotchas: Early stage — API changes between minor versions
Flutter dependency means larger binary sizes for desktop packaging
Python-Flutter bridge adds latency vs native Flutter
Mobile deployment requires Xcode/Android Studio setup
best_when: Internal tools needing web + desktop + mobile from pure Python
Data dashboards requiring reactive UI without JavaScript
Rapid prototyping of multi-platform Python apps
Teams wanting Flutter's UI quality without learning Dart
avoid_when: Production mobile apps (native Flutter or React Native better)
Data visualization dashboards (Streamlit/Plotly Dash more mature)
summary: Flet builds Flutter-powered web, desktop, and mobile apps with pure Python. ft.app(target=main) launches. ft.Text, ft.ElevatedButton, ft.Column, ft.DataTable — Flutter components via Python. Reactive callbacks: on_click, on_change. Async support. Multi-user shared apps. Deploy to web (flet publish), desktop, or mobile. Apache-2.0. Early stage but rapidly evolving. Better for internal tools than production mobile. For data apps use Streamlit or Dash.
